TOP OF THE TIMES


TODAY'S HEADLINES | WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 7, 2007


Top Stories
California/Local
National
World
Business
Sports
Arts and Entertainment
Features
Opinion




TOP STORIES [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/C4NBL/KI/h]


Single and not so restless in Cairo
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/2ODRS/KI/h]
By Jeffrey Fleishman
In Egypt, where most marry young to someone chosen by elders, many women are
flouting norms to pursue careers and becoming picky in their choice of spouse.

Key Pakistanis challenge Musharraf
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/KQB2V/KI/h]
By Laura King
The deposed Supreme Court chief justice and former Prime Minister Bhutto raise
stakes with opposition to emergency rule.

Voice for democracy muffled in Pakistan
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/IITCC/KI/h]
By Henry Chu
Revered activist Asma Jahangir, who's spent much of her life fighting for human
rights, is under house arrest.

Yahoo taken to task over China
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/C4NBG/KI/h]
By Jim Puzzanghera
Lawmakers lash out at the firm for giving the government names of e-mail account
holders, who were then jailed.

Strike about to cost jobs
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/HYL0V/KI/h]
By Joseph Menn, Claudia Eller and Richard Verrier
Studios move to halt production deals, which would hit rank and file.

A branch office of Berkeley's protest tradition
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/97JLB/KI/h]
By Thomas Bonk
The USC-Cal matchup has a rival -- the university versus activists perched high
in a grove slated for destruction. All they are saying is give trees a chance.

Inside Today's Times
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/B5FH6/KI/h]
Muksasey vote is sent to full Senate

More Top Stories [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/97JL8/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



CALIFORNIA/LOCAL [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/SP2XQ/KI/h]


Carona takes paid leave
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/IITC5/KI/h]
By Christine Hanley, David Reyes and H.g. Reza
The sheriff, who has been indicted on corruption charges, announces he will take
a paid 60-day leave of absence. He names an aide, Undersheriff Jo Ann Galisky,
as interim head of the agency.

$3 million doesn't buy what it used to
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/PF05E/KI/h]
By Steve Lopez
Sabrina Gordon-Gilardian grew up in the San Fernando Valley with her heart set
on living in one place, and one place only.

Governor teeters on edge of deficit abyss
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/IITCW/KI/h]
By Evan Halper
He talked about the state living within its means, but nothing changed. Now a
crisis threatens.

Police paint portrait of fatal melee in South L.A.
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/NJ67E/KI/h]
By Richard Winton and Jean-paul Renaud
Brawl began as an argument about a man, then quickly escalated into violence.

More California/Local News
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/62QP0/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



NATIONAL [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/JEH2Y/KI/h]


Allergan's net income climbs 48%
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/YB49H/KI/h]
Allergan Inc., the Irvine-based maker of the wrinkle smoother Botox, said
Tuesday that third-quarter profit rose 48% on sales of the product, breast
implants and eye drops.

White House, Congress in budget standoff
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/FKUOK/KI/h]
By Richard Simon
Disagreement over spending and other issues is heightening partisan tensions and
causing legislative gridlock. Compromise is unlikely as next year's campaign
season nears.

Bush urges FDA recall power
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/ZGWS8/KI/h]
By Ricardo Alonso- Zaldivar and James Gerstenzang
The new stance points to flaws in the current system. Critics welcome the
change, but contend it's short on specifics.

More National News [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/U1ZYT/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



WORLD [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/IITV9/KI/h]


Afghan suicide blast kills 42
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/R3VXL/KI/h]
By M. Karim Faiez and Henry Chu
Six lawmakers in a visiting delegation and children are among those slain in the
attack at a factory north of Kabul.

Benedict XVI meets with Saudi king in Vatican City
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/8ZSLW/KI/h]
By Maria De Cristofaro and Jeffrey Fleishman
In the first such talks, the leaders emphasize the 'importance of
collaboration.'

U.S. Army sniper on trial in Baghdad
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/5VEP5/KI/h]
By Ned Parker
The sergeant's lawyer says three deaths were justified. The military denies
planting weapons to draw Iraqi targets.

More World News [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/WTME0/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



BUSINESS [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/FKUOJ/KI/h]


Southland debut could be difficult for Fresh & Easy
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/U1ZY0/KI/h]
By Jerry Hirsch
The British grocery chain's philosophy may lure shoppers, but some activists
accuse the firm of breaking its promises.

Facebook strategy: You're the ad
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/JEH2U/KI/h]
By Jessica Guynn
The social network unveils a system to use members to promote products through
the site's news-feed feature.

IndyMac reports huge loss
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/FKUON/KI/h]
By E. Scott Reckard
The mortgage lender posts a deficit of $202.7 million in the third quarter, more
than five times what it had forecast.

$39-billion earnings charge by GM stuns Wall Street
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/WTMEZ/KI/h]
By Ken Bensinger
The move portends a record-breaking quarterly loss.

More Business News [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/L9GZB/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



SPORTS [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/JEH2H/KI/h]


UCLA's Westbrook has designs on being a cut above
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/U1ZY7/KI/h]
By Diane Pucin
From his 'flaming mohawk' to his flashy game, the sophomore guard seems poised
for a breakout year.

Use of instant replay is a call baseball should make
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/62QUS/KI/h]
By Bill Plaschke
Despite what purists claim, there are more positives than negatives to come from
it.

It's a long-distance fall
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/ZGWSD/KI/h]
By Mike Bresnahan
L.A. can't stop Hornets' Stojakovic, who makes 10 of 13 three-pointers and
scores 36 points. Paul sets him up with a team-record 21 assists.

More Sports News [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/KQBAO/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



ARTS AND ENTERTAINMENT
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/XH5QD/KI/h]


Dreaming up a 'Jeopardy!' answer is a union job, too
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/5VEPF/KI/h]
By From The Associated Press
In the entertainment industry, there's more to writing than putting words on the
page.

'Troy' is an odyssey of archaeologist's life
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/IITV0/KI/h]
By Tim Rutten
AT 58, Peter Ackroyd is one of those erudite English word machines whose career
you can call capacious and still feel as if you're understating things.

Striking writers take a page from the past
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/U1ZYD/KI/h]
By Jay A. Fernandez
FOR an industry at war over competing visions of the digital-age future, the
sight this week of thousands of Writers Guild members whipping out their
bluntest, most antiquated weapon -- picketing -- was, well, striking. Especially
for a professional group typically wary of physical movement and potential
exposure to sunlight.

More Arts and Entertainment News
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/JEH22/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



FEATURES [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/PF04W/KI/h]


Plateau de fruits de mer: Extravagance on the half shell
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/B5FB8/KI/h]
By Betty Hallock
On the town, it's quite the event. Made to order at home, it can dazzle even
more.

Chilled seafood's perfect partner: Chablis
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/IITVZ/KI/h]
By Patrick Comiskey
Warmer weather has put the crisp white on a ripe new path.

The green room [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/XH5QR/KI/h]
By Susan Carpenter
Hey, did you hear the one about Jay Leno and his eco-friendly Burbank garage?

More Features [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/SP2KQ/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------



OPINION [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/SP2K9/KI/h]


Are the corporate suits ruining TV?
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/5VEPG/KI/h]
By Marshall Herskovitz
Network control and media consolidation are wringing the creativity out of
entertainment.

Keeping innovators out of the country
[http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/C4NGA/KI/h]
By Andrés Martinez
Low quotas and high fees mean the U.S. is unable to lure the best foreign
talent.

USC's O.J. mess [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/62QUO/KI/h]
By Scott Olin Schmidt
The university still honor's Simpson's football career, sending players the
wrong message.

More Opinion [http://link.latimes.com/r/TWOAU6/D9N5/L9JPA0/CL5/4CI36/KI/h]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
